title: arctostaphylos crustacea subsp. rosei
intro: Arctostaphylos crustacea subsp. rosei, commonly known as the pink manzanita, is a plant species native to the Pacific coast of North America, characterized by its pink or reddish flowers and leathery leaves. The pink manzanita has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as protecting against cardiovascular disease, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the pink manzanita, typically in the form of teas, tinctures, or infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
